title: veronica dissecta subsp. dissecta
intro: Veronica dissecta subsp. dissecta, also known as the cut-leaved speedwell or dissected speedwell, is a plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate sinus congestion. Veronica dissecta subsp. dissecta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, often combined with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
